providerExtensions.name: AGENTS.md Filename
providerExtensions.description: Codex loads AI instruction rules from a file named AGENTS.md (or a configurable fallback list of filenames set via config.toml's project_doc_fallback_filenames). The file is discovered by walking up from the current working directory. Multiple AGENTS.md files can exist in a hierarchy — project root, subdirectory, and home directory — and their contents are combined. The primary file is AGENTS.md; fallbacks (e.g. .agents.md, CLAUDE.md) are checked when AGENTS.md is absent.

providerExtensions.name: Hierarchical AGENTS.md (child_agents_md feature flag)
providerExtensions.description: When the child_agents_md feature flag is enabled (via [features] in config.toml), Codex appends additional guidance about AGENTS.md scope and precedence to the user instructions message, and emits that message even when no AGENTS.md is present. This implements a multi-file hierarchical rules model where rules from parent directories, project root, and home directory can be combined with scoped precedence.

providerExtensions.name: Project Doc Max Bytes
providerExtensions.description: A configurable cap on how many bytes are read from an AGENTS.md file, set via project_doc_max_bytes in config.toml. Prevents oversized rules files from consuming excessive context window.
